steps.title: Punctuate the appositive
steps.content: The phrase in this case, Henry Cavendish adds identifying information inside the noun phrase. Because it interrupts the sentence before the relative clause who was not the first, it needs matching punctuation on both sides.

steps.title: Reject mismatched punctuation
steps.content: Choice A opens with a colon and closes with a comma. Choice B opens with a dash and closes with a comma. Choice C has no opening punctuation for the appositive. Only matching dashes correctly bracket the interruption. The answer is D.
